headline: Transcript: Kennedy injects uncertainty into big pharma
summary: Sonja Hutson talks to Oliver Barnes and Alice Hancock
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Kennedy injects uncertainty into big pharma’

Sonja Hutson Good morning from the Financial Times. Today is Tuesday, November 26th. And this is your FT News Briefing.

Two major criminal cases against Donald Trump are ending. And we look at how big pharma will fare under the incoming US administration. Plus, a new EU rule will allow the bloc to peek under the hood of Chinese electric vehicles. I’m Sonja Hutson, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

The US Department of Justice is dropping two federal criminal cases against Donald Trump. One accused the incoming president of interfering with the 2020 election. The other alleged that he mishandled classified documents. Special counsel Jack Smith said in a court filing yesterday that continuing the cases would violate a long-standing DOJ policy against prosecuting a sitting president. The filing related to the election interference charge asks
